Neksus JobSpec (neksus-jobspec)

What it is
- Open, local-first CLI and Python package for structured, branded,
  machine-readable job campaigns.
- Free/core scope only: define -> validate -> render -> export -> document
  JobSpec files.

Product boundary (important)
- This package does NOT collect applications, upload/parse CVs, send emails,
  process payments, or manage candidates.
- This package does NOT include hosted SaaS features, LinkedIn API posting,
  ATS automation, dashboards, or backend infrastructure.

Schema basics (v0.3.0)
- Top-level fields: `schema_version`, `id`, `page`, `job`, `components`,
  optional `campaign`, optional `rendering`.
- `campaign`:
  - `starts_at: date | optional`
  - `expires_at: date | optional`
  - `status: draft | active | expired | closed | optional`
- `job.apply` (strict method-based):
  - `method: email | external_url | ats_url | custom | agent_ready`
  - email method requires `email`
  - URL methods require `url`
  - `agent_ready` requires `url` and `job_reference`

Minimal YAML
```yaml
schema_version: 1
id: backend-engineer
page:
  layout: job_detail
job:
  title: Backend Engineer
  apply:
    method: external_url
    url: https://example.com/apply/backend-engineer
components:
  - type: list
    id: requirements
    items:
      - 3+ years backend experience
```

Common CLI commands
- `neksus-jobspec init`
- `neksus-jobspec spec validate <path>`
- `neksus-jobspec spec render <path> --format web`
- `neksus-jobspec spec render <path> --format json-ld`
- `neksus-jobspec spec export <path> --target generic-json --out dist/job.json`
- `neksus-jobspec spec export <path> --target generic-xml --out dist/job.xml`
- `neksus-jobspec spec export <path> --target linkedin-ready-json --out dist/linkedin-job.json`
- `neksus-jobspec feed export "examples/*.jobspec.yaml" --target jobs-json --out dist/jobs.json`
- `neksus-jobspec feed sitemap "examples/*.jobspec.yaml" --base-url https://company.dk/jobs --out dist/sitemap.xml`

Optional local MCP
- Install: `pip install "neksus-jobspec[mcp]"`
- Run: `neksus-jobspec-mcp`
- Scope: local stdio, CLI/API-parity tools only.
- Not included: application intake, CV handling, payments, hosted APIs,
  ATS workflows, LinkedIn API posting.

Common mistakes
- Using legacy `job.apply: {label, url}` instead of method-based apply.
- Adding unsupported fields like `application_form`, CV/candidate data,
  or hosted backend concepts.
- Using unknown component types/variants.
- Skipping validation before render/export.
